feature_name: v1.22.0 "Player Parity + Roadmap": TOP-PRIORITY mobile-player regression fix (native controls restored on mobile VIDEO, v1.21 custom bar kept on desktop + mobile audio, old gesture layer restored with a text-selection fix); retroactive Subscribe-button backfill from download-folder identity; creator-name link to /subscriptions; era-theme light-mode contrast audit; desktop-sidebar channel pins (v1.21 FR-5 follow-up); configurable max-duration download gate (default 2h); loop/repeat toggle; desktop cross-tab playback + native Picture-in-Picture; card-level download-to-device; watch-page scroll-jump re-verification
base: main (v1.21.0). Prior shipped records live in docs/exec-plans/completed/ (most recently v1.21-polish-release).
process_plan: Discovery completed by product-manager, grounded directly in ROADMAP.md's v1.22 candidate list (every FR below already listed there -- confirms no scope creep), docs/ui-research-2026-07.md, the completed 2026-07-06-v1.16-watch-experience.md (persistent-player/dock architecture FR-1 must not break) and 2026-07-08-v1.21-polish-release.md (custom control-bar FR-1 partially reverses on mobile video) exec plans, and the live code (public/js/player.js in full, public/watch.html/index.html/setup.html/lib/ytdlp/views/subscriptions.html's #player-host-template, public/css/style.css's player/control-bar/theme-token sections, server.js's scan merge + freshlyScannedIds channel-identity bridge, lib/ytdlp/args.js/index.js/store.js/url.js, public/js/common.js/main.js/watch.js/setup.js). Ten FRs, each grounded with a specific code-reading finding (see the exec plan's Decision log): FR-1's root cause (v1.21 FR-2 removed native `controls` app-wide including mobile video, where it regresses fullscreen/speed/download/AirPlay/PiP + causes a text-selection glitch during hold-2x -- no `user-select`/`-webkit-touch-callout` exists anywhere on the player surface); FR-2's root cause (channel-identity bridging in server.js only ever runs on a file's FIRST scan via `freshlyScannedIds` -- an already-indexed item is never revisited, explaining both the pre-v1.20 gap and a suspected periodic-auto-scan race for the subscription-path gap); FR-5's scope (THREE independently duplicated `renderSidebarFolders` implementations in main.js/watch.js/setup.js, since `#sidebar-folders-list` lives outside `#view-root` in the persistent shell); FR-6's pattern to mirror exactly (`DEFAULT_MAX_VIDEOS`/`validateMaxVideos`/`effectiveMaxVideos` in config.js/store.js/args.js); FR-4's finding (every Subscribe-adjacent CSS surface is already token-driven, no hardcoded colors -- this is a token-VALUE audit, not a missing-token bug); FR-10's finding (the v1.17.0 fix -- `--mobile-header-h`/`--mobile-bottom-nav-h` -- is confirmed still in place, zero `position:sticky` anywhere in style.css -- this is a re-verification, not presumed-still-broken work). Six minor forks resolved non-blocking (see product_forks_for_dean); no genuinely blocking fork surfaced. Review tiers: HEAVIEST two-reviewer/adversarial gate for FR-1 (persistent-player regression risk, Dean iOS on-device arbiter); two-reviewer gate for FR-2 (data + spawn-guard-adjacent), FR-5 (folders/config invariant), FR-6 (yt-dlp args/spawn); LIGHT-TO-MEDIUM for FR-8; LIGHT single-QA for FR-3, FR-4, FR-7, FR-9, FR-10 (FR-4 has Dean's on-device contrast pass as arbiter). FR-1 is flagged independently shippable first for fast regression relief (no file-overlap dependency on the rest of the round except FR-8's later PiP-button touch on the same #player-controls bar).
hard_constraints: Additive / no regressions: FR-1 must preserve every other v1.16/v1.21 player feature (dock FULL/DOCKED/CLOSED, prev/next, autoplay-next, transcode + resume overlays, Media Session, progress saving, audio-bg-art) across the new native-mobile-video/custom-everywhere-else split -- the persistent <video id="media-player"> element and its one-time wireHostListeners() model are unchanged in kind; only controls-attribute presence + #player-controls visibility become conditional.
Desktop and mobile-audio custom-bar behavior must be byte-identical to v1.21 -- no regression to what Dean already likes. Only mobile video changes.

Disabled-yt-dlp-module byte-identical guarantee preserved for FR-2/FR-3/FR-5/FR-6: with FILETUBE_YTDLP_ENABLED off, no new route/UI/DOM is reachable; /subscriptions still 404s server-side exactly as today.

db.folders/folderSettings synthetic-root invariant unchanged (FR-5): a pin is NEVER written into db.folders or folderSettings; the pin store is never scanned/pruned as a folder. FR-5's sidebar rendering is a read-only consumer of the existing v1.21 pin store/routes -- no new write surface.

No existing security guard weakened: FR-2's inferred channelUrl passes the SAME, unmodified validateChannelUrl before use (defense-in-depth re-validation, not blind trust one hop removed); FR-6's --match-filter value is always a re-validated bounded integer interpolated into a fixed-shape string, never raw user text, passed via the existing spawn argv array (never a shell).

Follow docs/CONTRIBUTING.md: vanilla DOM (no framework/bundler), CommonJS server, 2-space/semicolons/single-quotes, textContent over innerHTML for new dynamic strings (FR-3's channel link, FR-5's sidebar pin labels, FR-9's card button), node:test for new/regression tests, lint 0 warnings, NO new runtime dependencies.

Preserve the era-theme system (CSS custom properties) for all new/changed CSS: FR-1's mobile-video area + gesture-surface user-select fix, FR-4's contrast fixes (may change token VALUES per era/light combo, never introduce a hardcoded color bypassing the token system), FR-5's sidebar pins, FR-8's PiP button.

Node.js 22 LTS (engines >=20); no headless-browser/E2E infrastructure exists (docs/RELIABILITY.md) -- Dean's on-device pass (especially iOS Safari) is the documented arbiter for FR-1 (mobile feel, native-vs-custom split, gesture layer), FR-4 (contrast), and FR-8 (PiP feel) -- not a testing gap.
FR-1's and FR-8(a)'s mobile-detection signal must be the SAME named, pure, reused helper -- never two divergent 'is this mobile' implementations across the round.

product_forks_for_dean.fr1_mobile_detection_signal: RESOLVED (recommendation, PE owns exact heuristic) -- combine viewport width with a coarse-pointer/no-hover media-query or touch-capability check (the only existing signal, isMobileViewport()'s bare matchMedia(max-width:768px), is explicitly called out by Dean as insufficient since the same shell serves a narrowed desktop window and a large tablet). Must be exposed as one named, unit-testable pure helper reused by both FR-1 and FR-8(a).
product_forks_for_dean.fr2_backfill_never_overwrite: RESOLVED, not a genuine fork -- the folder-based backfill only ever fills a genuine gap (item has no channelUrl yet); it must never overwrite an item that already has a captured identity from the existing freshlyScannedIds bridge or a prior backfill.
product_forks_for_dean.fr3_deep_link_target: RESOLVED (recommendation) -- deep-link to the specific channel/subscription when identity is resolvable (reusing watch.js's existing decideSubscribeButtonState resolution); otherwise fall back to the general /subscriptions page. Either outcome is safe; not blocking.
product_forks_for_dean.fr6_default_and_sentinel: RESOLVED -- 7200 seconds (2h) default, exactly as specified; 0/blank = unbounded, mirroring DEFAULT_MAX_VIDEOS's existing 0-means-unlimited semantics precisely (no new sentinel invented).
product_forks_for_dean.fr7_persistence_mechanism: RESOLVED (recommendation, PE may revise) -- localStorage (mirroring the v1.21 FR-9 theatre-mode ft-theater pattern), not a server db.settings setting -- loop is a watch-page-local preference like theatre mode, not a cross-device Settings-page preference like autoplayNext.
product_forks_for_dean.fr8a_desktop_scoping: RESOLVED, not a genuine fork -- reuses FR-1's mobile-detection helper (fork #1) rather than a separate signal or a new user-facing setting, so 'what counts as mobile' is provably identical across both features.

FR-1.title: [TOP PRIORITY, heaviest] Diverging-but-cohesive controls: native on mobile VIDEO, custom on desktop + mobile audio
FR-1.review_weight: HEAVIEST two-reviewer/adversarial gate this round -- persistent-player regression risk spans the whole v1.16 FULL/DOCKED/CLOSED shell + every player feature (dock, prev/next, autoplay-next, transcode/resume overlays, Media Session, progress saving). Dean's iOS on-device pass is the arbiter for feel.
files: public/index.html, public/setup.html, public/watch.html, lib/ytdlp/views/subscriptions.html (#player-host-template, byte-identical across all four)
public/css/style.css (#player-controls / mobile-video / gesture-surface user-select sections, era tokens)
test/unit (mobile-detection helper, any control-surface decision helper)

FR-2.title: Retroactive Subscribe-button backfill from download-folder identity
FR-2.review_weight: Two-reviewer gate (db.metadata mutation + spawn-guard-adjacent surface via validateChannelUrl reuse).
files: server.js (scan merge -- new backfill block, sibling to the existing freshlyScannedIds-scoped bridge)
lib/ytdlp/index.js (enrichSubscriptionWithChannelDir reuse; investigate/fix the periodic-scan race)
test/unit + test/integration (backfill matcher, never-overwrite invariant, validateChannelUrl reuse)

FR-3.title: Subscriber/creator name -> subscriptions page link
FR-3.review_weight: LIGHT single-QA gate -- display/navigation-only, reuses already-validated identity resolution.

FR-4.title: Grey-on-white contrast in light mode on certain era themes
FR-4.review_weight: LIGHT single-QA gate -- CSS token-value audit only (every relevant surface already token-driven, no missing-token bug). On-device Dean pass is the arbiter.

FR-5.title: Pins in the desktop sidebar (v1.21 FR-5 follow-up)
FR-5.review_weight: Two-reviewer gate (data-safety / folders-config invariant) -- must reprove db.folders/folderSettings are never touched; this task is a read-only consumer of the existing pin store.
files: public/js/main.js, public/js/watch.js, public/js/setup.js (all three duplicated renderSidebarFolders implementations)
test/unit + test/integration (regression: pin store never touches db.folders/folderSettings)

FR-6.title: Max-duration download gate (parameter, default 2h, changeable like max-videos)

FR-7.title: Loop / repeat toggle
FR-7.review_weight: LIGHT gate -- additive, isolated to the existing 'ended' handler cluster in player.js plus one watch-page toggle.
files: public/js/player.js ('ended' handler reconciliation with autoplayNext, live-transcode restart path)

FR-8.title: Desktop cross-tab video + native Picture-in-Picture
FR-8.review_weight: LIGHT-TO-MEDIUM gate. Dean's on-device pass (desktop browser) is the arbiter for PiP feel.
files: public/js/player.js (shouldPauseForLifecycleEvent scoping reusing FR-1's mobile helper; new PiP button wiring in #player-controls)

FR-9.title: Card-level download-to-device
FR-9.review_weight: LIGHT gate -- pure UI addition reusing the existing, unmodified /video/:id?download=1 route (no new server route).
files: public/js/main.js (card template + download button, isolated from thumbnail-container's anchor)

FR-10.title: Watch-page scroll-jump -- verify + fix if still repro
FR-10.review_weight: PROCESS/LIGHT -- grounded finding: the v1.17.0 fix (--mobile-header-h/--mobile-bottom-nav-h) is confirmed still in place, zero position:sticky anywhere in style.css. This is a re-verification; only becomes code work if Dean's on-device pass finds a NEW reproduction.
files: public/css/style.css (only if AC69 finds a new reproduction)

pe_design_items: Route to Design after Discovery. All FRs except FR-4/FR-10 need PE design (FR-4/FR-10 ride through Design for one cohesive plan but are trivial/scoped -- a token-value audit and a re-verification respectively). Key open engineering questions for PE, in order of weight: (1) FR-1's exact combined mobile-detection heuristic (product fork #1), the exact controls-attribute/#player-controls-visibility toggle mechanism consistent with the one-time wireHostListeners() model, confirming wireSkipHoldGestures(mediaPlayer)'s existing wiring needs NO change, the exact user-select/-webkit-touch-callout CSS fix scope, #fs-btn/#vol-bar conditional presence across the four form-factor x media-type cases, and a proof that dock/prev-next/autoplay-next/overlays/Media Session/progress-saving are unaffected by the toggle; (2) FR-2's exact backfill matcher mechanics (folder-path-to-channelDir comparison, likely reusing enrichSubscriptionWithChannelDir) and confirming/fixing the suspected periodic-scan race; (3) FR-6's exact --match-filter combination mechanics when skip-Shorts AND max-duration are both active (verify yt-dlp's real multi-filter behavior, don't assume) plus the settings-sheet/add-form field additions; (4) FR-5's de-duplicate-vs-three-parallel-additions call for the three renderSidebarFolders implementations (recommend the smaller/lower-risk parallel-additions option unless duplication cost is judged worth paying down now); (5) FR-7's exact 'ended'-handler reconciliation ordering (loop must short-circuit before handleAutoplayNext) and its pure decision helper; (6) FR-8's PiP button placement/feature-detect gating in #player-controls and confirming reuse of FR-1's mobile-detection helper for the pause-on-hidden scoping; (7) FR-3/FR-9 are comparatively direct. See the exec plan's ## Flag for the principal-engineer section for full detail. FR-1 is flagged as independently shippable/verifiable first (no file-overlap dependency on the rest of the round except FR-8's later #player-controls touch) -- EM may choose to sequence it as a fast-relief first wave.
cross_cutting: Disabled-yt-dlp-module no-op preserved end-to-end for FR-2/FR-3/FR-5/FR-6 (no new route/UI/DOM reachable with FILETUBE_YTDLP_ENABLED off; FR-2's backfill/FR-5's sidebar-pin fetch/FR-6's duration gate never run when disabled). Era-theme CSS vars for all new/changed styling (FR-1/FR-4/FR-5/FR-8). No new npm dependencies; CommonJS server; vanilla DOM client code; node:test covers every new pure helper; lint 0 warnings. textContent (never innerHTML) for every new dynamic string (FR-3/FR-5/FR-9). Node 22 toolchain throughout. No headless-browser/E2E infrastructure exists (docs/RELIABILITY.md) -- Dean's on-device pass is the documented arbiter for FR-1 (mobile feel/iOS)/FR-4 (contrast)/FR-8 (PiP feel), not a testing gap. FR-1's and FR-8(a)'s mobile-detection signal must be the SAME reused helper, never two divergent implementations. Review tiers: HEAVIEST for FR-1; two-reviewer for FR-2/FR-5/FR-6; LIGHT-TO-MEDIUM for FR-8; LIGHT single-QA for FR-3/FR-4/FR-7/FR-9/FR-10. FR-1 is independently shippable first for fast regression relief.

coordinator_decisions.fr3_link_target: General /subscriptions fallback link only (no channel deep-link this round).
coordinator_decisions.fr6_field_unit: SECONDS (mirrors maxVideos); env default FILETUBE_YTDLP_MAX_DURATION_SECONDS=7200 (2h); 0/blank = unbounded.
coordinator_decisions.fr2_creator_display: Satisfied by resolveChannelName precedence once backfill writes channelName (AC80); save-to-device button is a regression-lock (AC81), no code change expected.
coordinator_decisions.fr1_mute_slash_nit: Folded into FR-1's T2 style.css task (owns the .mute-icon-off::after block).
coordinator_decisions.fr1_t1_t2_split: FR-1 split into T1 (player.js logic) + T2 (style.css). Disjoint files; T2 is written against T1's frozen .ff-mobile contract; both HEAVIEST gate + Dean iOS arbiter.
coordinator_decisions.git_ownership: Coordinator owns ALL git. SDEs report files-changed + Node 22 test/lint output and DO NOT commit.

wave_plan.principle: Shared working tree: no two CONCURRENT tasks may edit the same file. The SERVER track (TA on server.js/lib/ytdlp/index.js, TB on the rest of lib/ytdlp/*) is fully disjoint from each other and from the CLIENT track, so both run in parallel in Wave 1. Client hot-file order enforced: player.js = T1 -> TF -> TG ; style.css = T2 -> TC -> TE -> TF -> TG -> TH ; watch.js = TC -> TD -> TF ; watch.html = TD -> TF -> TG ; main.js = TC -> TH.
wave_plan.wave3_disjointness_note: TE touches only style.css; TD touches only watch.html + watch.js -> disjoint, safe to run concurrently.
wave_plan.process_track: TI (FR-10 scroll-jump re-verify) is Dean on-device, no code, no file lock -- may run any time; nominally slotted in Wave 2.

tasks.title: Player controls-mode logic + shared form-factor helper
tasks.description: Add resolveMobileFormFactor/isMobileFormFactor, resolveControlsMode, applyControlsMode(); wire applyControlsMode into mountInSlot/dock; removeAttribute('controls') in teardownMediaState; export the two pure helpers; add test/unit/player-form-factor.test.js (AC1, AC10) with a regression lock on the four-way table. Leave wireSkipHoldGestures and isMobileViewport untouched.
tasks.gate: HEAVIEST two-reviewer/adversarial + Dean iOS on-device arbiter
tasks.done_when: Pure helpers exported + unit-tested; controls attribute/.ff-mobile toggled only in applyControlsMode/teardownMediaState; wireHostListeners unchanged; Node 22 lint + tests pass.

tasks.title: Responsive-controls CSS + text-selection fix + mute-slash nit
tasks.description: Add the /* === v1.22 FR-1 === */ style.css section: user-select/-webkit-touch-callout guards on the gesture surfaces (AC6); hide the custom bar on mobile video (AC4); drop #vol-bar+#mute-btn on mobile audio (AC3). Also fix the muted-state diagonal-slash mis-position in .mute-icon-off::after (folded-in nit). No new color tokens; do not touch the v1.21 FR-2 section.
tasks.gate: HEAVIEST two-reviewer/adversarial + Dean iOS on-device arbiter
tasks.done_when: New FR-1 CSS section added reacting to .ff-mobile/.audio-mode per the design's schematic; mute-slash re-anchored; era tokens only; Node 22 lint passes.

tasks.title: Retroactive folder-based Subscribe-button backfill
tasks.description: Add pure matchChannelDirToSubscription(filePath, resolvedSubs) (exact parent-dir equality, not prefix) + ytdlp.backfillChannelIdentityFromFolder(fresh, item, config) wrapper (resolvedSubs memoized once/scan via enrichSubscriptionWithChannelDir; re-validate matched channelUrl via url.validateChannelUrl) in lib/ytdlp/index.js; add a sibling backfill block in server.js Phase-2 mutator NOT scoped to freshlyScannedIds, never overwriting an existing channelUrl, scoped to ytdlpDownloadRoots. Backfill writes channelName (from sub.name) for creator display (AC80). Add regression-lock that save-to-device button stays ungated (AC81). Unit + integration.
tasks.done_when: Pure matcher unit-tested (exact match; prefix rejected; never-overwrite); integration: identity-less yt-dlp item under channelDir gets channelUrl+channelName on scan, no yt-dlp spawn; validateChannelUrl re-validation; disabled-module no-op; Node 22 tests + lint pass.

tasks.title: Max-duration download gate (default 2h, mirrors maxVideos)
tasks.description: config.js: DEFAULT_MAX_DURATION_SECONDS=7200 + parseMaxDurationSeconds (0 = valid unbounded, invalid/unset -> 7200) + parse FILETUBE_YTDLP_MAX_DURATION_SECONDS into config.maxDurationSeconds. store.js: MAX_SUB_MAX_DURATION_SECONDS + validateMaxDurationSeconds mirroring validateMaxVideos, wired into validateSubscriptionInput/Patch/addSubscription/updateSubscription. args.js: effectiveMaxDurationSeconds + pure buildMatchFilterArg(clauses) that AND-joins SHORTS_MATCH_FILTER and 'duration < <n>' into ONE --match-filter with ' & ' (yt-dlp OR-combines separate flags -- verified). UI: expose maxDurationSeconds (SECONDS) in the settings sheet + add-form. Unit tests.
tasks.done_when: parse/validate/effective/buildMatchFilterArg unit-tested (both-active AND-join, each-alone, neither -> omit); UI field added; bounded integer in fixed-shape string via spawn argv; Node 22 tests + lint pass.

tasks.title: Pins in the desktop sidebar (shared renderPinnedSidebar)
tasks.description: Add ONE shared renderPinnedSidebar(pins) to common.js mirroring renderPinnedPlaylists (reuse derivePinnedPlaylistEntries; createElement/textContent/createTextNode only). Insert a SEPARATE sibling #sidebar-pinned-section after #sidebar-folders-list (never a child). Each of main.js/watch.js/setup.js on init() fetches GET /api/subscriptions/pins (404 -> []) and calls renderPinnedSidebar (idempotent). Small #sidebar-pinned-section CSS (era tokens). NEVER writes db.folders/folderSettings. Unit + integration invariant lock.
tasks.done_when: Shared pin renderer reused at all three call sites; sibling section survives folder-list innerHTML rebuilds; db.folders/folderSettings never touched (regression-locked); disabled-module -> folders-only; Node 22 tests + lint pass.

tasks.title: Creator name -> /subscriptions SPA link (general fallback)
tasks.description: Change #uploader-channel-name from <div> to <a class="uploader-channel-link"> (no href by default). populateMetadata keeps setting .textContent. In setupSubscribeButton, set link.href = '/subscriptions' when module enabled (general fallback ONLY -- no deep-link, per coordinator decision); leave href unset when disabled/no identity. Pure resolveUploaderLinkHref({moduleEnabled}) unit-test target. textContent/.href-safe, never innerHTML.
tasks.done_when: <a> navigates via SPA router to /subscriptions with no reload; inert plain text when no identity; pure href helper unit-tested; Node 22 tests + lint pass.

tasks.title: Era-theme light-mode contrast token-value audit/fix
tasks.description: Audit each era's [data-mode="light"] block for grey-on-white/low-contrast (neutral .btn/--btn-bg + --text-primary; --text-secondary; the 'Subscribed' neutral button Dean flagged). Fix by adjusting the offending CSS custom-property VALUE in that specific era x light block only -- never a hardcoded color, never touching dark mode or a non-flagged era's light mode. Dean on-device arbiter for final values.
tasks.done_when: Offending era x light token values darkened/adjusted; no dark-mode or non-flagged-era regression; token system preserved; Node 22 lint passes.

tasks.title: Loop / repeat toggle
tasks.description: Pure resolveEndedAction({loop, autoplayNext, hasNext}) -> 'repeat'|'advance'|'stop' (loop always wins), exported for unit test. isLoopEnabled()/setLoop() via localStorage['ft-loop'] (guarded, theatre-mode pattern). Add a loop 'ended' listener that replays THIS item (startLiveStream(0,true) for live; else currentTime=0 + play()); gate handleAutoplayNext with early 'if (isLoopEnabled()) return;' so loop short-circuits before autoplay-advance. Watch-page toggle button (setupLoopToggle in watch.js, mirroring setupAutoplayToggle but localStorage). Reuse autoplay-toggle CSS class (likely no new CSS).
tasks.done_when: resolveEndedAction unit-tested (loop precedence); loop replays this item incl. live-transcode; loop-off byte-identical to today; persists across reload; Node 22 tests + lint pass.

tasks.title: Desktop cross-tab video + native Picture-in-Picture
tasks.description: (a) Scope pause-on-hidden to mobile: add isMobile to shouldPauseForLifecycleEvent ctx, video branch terminal 'return true' -> 'return !!ctx.isMobile'; call site handleBackgroundLifecycle adds isMobile: isMobileFormFactor() (REUSE FR-1's helper, AC78). Unit-test new signature. (b) Add <button id="pip-btn" class="pc-btn pip-btn"> to #player-controls in #player-host-template -- BYTE-IDENTICAL across ALL FOUR shells (index.html, setup.html, watch.html, lib/ytdlp/views/subscriptions.html). Wire ONCE in wireHostListeners; feature-detect document.pictureInPictureEnabled, hide in .audio-mode; toggle requestPictureInPicture/exitPictureInPicture. Small .pip-btn CSS (era tokens).
tasks.done_when: Desktop video keeps playing across tab switches; mobile pause-on-hidden byte-identical (unit-regression); PiP button feature-detected, four-shell byte-identical (parity-test); reuses isMobileFormFactor; Node 22 tests + lint pass.

tasks.title: Card-level download-to-device
tasks.description: Add a .card-download-btn overlay anchor to main.js's card template as a SIBLING of .thumbnail-container's <a> (never nested -- no watch navigation, AC64), mirroring .card-delete-btn placement/event-isolation. Reuse the existing /video/${id}?download=1 route unchanged; href id via encodeURIComponent, download filename hint escaped; textContent/.href-safe. Small .card-download-btn CSS mirroring .card-delete-btn (era tokens). Unit-test pure href/filename builder + isolation.
tasks.done_when: Card download button reuses ?download=1, does not navigate to watch, works for yt-dlp + local items, href/filename-safe; Node 22 tests + lint pass.

tasks.title: Watch-page scroll-jump re-verification (process/no-code)
tasks.description: PROCESS task, no SDE code. Dean re-verifies on-device that the bottom-panel scroll-jump no longer reproduces (v1.17.0 --mobile-header-h/--mobile-bottom-nav-h fix confirmed in place; zero position:sticky repo-wide). If it does NOT reproduce -> close as resolved, no code change (AC70). If it DOES -> new finding, scope a small follow-up (AC71).
tasks.done_when: Dean confirms on-device; documented as resolved (no code) or scoped as a new follow-up.

history.note: PRODUCT-MANAGER completed Discovery for v1.22.0 'Player Parity + Roadmap' (feature/v1.22-player-parity, off main at v1.21.0) -- a 10-FR release LEADING with the top-priority mobile-player regression fix (FR-1), then working through the rest of Dean's post-v1.21 ROADMAP.md v1.22 candidate list. Read docs/CONTRIBUTING.md, docs/ARCHITECTURE.md, docs/RELIABILITY.md first, then the completed 2026-07-06-v1.16-watch-experience.md (persistent-player/dock architecture) and 2026-07-08-v1.21-polish-release.md (custom control-bar introduction, the FR-1 regression's origin) exec plans, docs/ui-research-2026-07.md, and ROADMAP.md's v1.22 candidate list (confirmed every FR below already listed there -- no scope creep). Grounded every FR directly in the live code: public/js/player.js in full (ensureHost/wireHostListeners one-time-wiring model, isMobileViewport()'s existing bare-width signal, wireSkipHoldGestures's existing unconditional #media-player wiring, enterFullscreen's desktop-retarget/iOS-native branch, shouldPauseForLifecycleEvent + its three lifecycle listeners, the 'ended' listener cluster); public/watch.html's #player-host-template (confirmed byte-identical across all four shells via grep) and public/css/style.css's player/control-bar sections (confirmed NO user-select/-webkit-touch-callout anywhere on the player surface -- grounds FR-1's text-selection fix); server.js's scan merge ~1360-1620 (the freshlyScannedIds-scoped channel-identity bridge -- grounds FR-2's root cause: an already-indexed item is never revisited); lib/ytdlp/args.js (resolveChannelDir, sanitizeChannelName, playlistEndArgs, SHORTS_MATCH_FILTER's fixed-literal --match-filter pattern -- grounds FR-6); lib/ytdlp/index.js (runSubscriptionCycle's persistCapturedChannelMeta + survivor-fallback recording, the already-exported enrichSubscriptionWithChannelDir -- grounds FR-2's backfill building blocks and the suspected periodic-scan race); lib/ytdlp/store.js (validateMaxVideos/MAX_SUB_MAX_VIDEOS -- the pattern FR-6 mirrors); lib/ytdlp/url.js in full (validateChannelUrl -- the guard FR-2's backfilled value must still pass); public/js/common.js (renderPinnedPlaylists/derivePinnedPlaylistEntries/restoreHomeFromCache's #sidebar-folders-list-lives-outside-#view-root finding); public/js/main.js/watch.js/setup.js (confirmed THREE independent renderSidebarFolders implementations -- grounds FR-5's scope); public/js/watch.js (#uploader-channel-name is a <div> not a link -- grounds FR-3; downloadBtn's existing ?download=1 pattern -- grounds FR-9); public/css/style.css's theme token blocks + Subscribe-adjacent CSS (all token-driven, no hardcoded colors found -- grounds FR-4 as a token-VALUE audit) and the --mobile-header-h/--mobile-bottom-nav-h v1.17.0 fix (grounds FR-10 as a re-verification, zero position:sticky repo-wide). No genuinely blocking fork surfaced; six minor forks resolved with a recommendation (see product_forks_for_dean).
